extraction.one_line_summary: PORTE Richie won Tour de Suisse GC after strong individual time trial on Stage 9, holding off resurgent FUGLSANG Jakob by 1:02.
tactical_patterns: FUGLSANG jumped from outside podium to 2nd overall via TT, clawing back Astana's 75-second team time trial deficit.
BMC's team cohesion facing sponsor loss translated to synchronized domestique support, with KÜNG and VAN AVERMAET sacrificing individual results.
QUINTANA lost 2nd place via 55-second TT deficit to PORTE, exposing vulnerability heading into Tour de France time trials.

rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: positive: Won Dauphiné last year, now 2nd at Suisse via exceptional TT form—pattern suggests peaks at Tour warm-up races, not Tour itself.
rider_signals.ANDERSON Søren Kragh: positive: 23-year-old finished 2nd in 35km TT after stage win earlier—Sunweb developing another high-wattage all-rounder like DUMOULIN.
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: 4th place in 35km TT shocked analysts given sprinter profile—suggests unrealized potential in longer time trials versus prologues.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: Crumbled in final stages, 86th in TT—either sandbagging for Tour or genuinely off-form, concerning for Movistar's three-pronged strategy.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Dropped to 3rd overall, 38th in TT—Tour cobbles and multiple TTs will compound this weakness against in-form rivals.
